title: Open Innovation and AI will define the next generation of manufacturing: Annika Olme, CTO, SKF
description: As global manufacturing enters an era shaped by AI, intelligent automation, and sustainability, the role of R&D is undergoing a profound shift. Innovation is no longer confined to centralized global hubs. Increasingly, companies are building distributed innovation ecosystems where engineering, digital intelligence, and product development happen closer to markets, customers, and talent pools. India has emerged at the centre of this transformation. What was once seen primarily as an execution destination is now becoming a strategic nerve centre for advanced engineering, industrial AI, and next-generation manufacturing innovation. For global industrial technology leader SKF, this evolution is playing out through deeper investments in engineering, AI-led industrial intelligence, digital manufacturing, and open innovation. From predictive maintenance and autonomous condition monitoring to AI-native manufacturing systems and sustainability-driven industrial design, SKF is redefining how industrial enterprises build resilience, efficiency, and scalability in a rapidly changing world. The post Open Innovation and AI will define the next generation of manufacturing: Annika Olme, CTO, SKF appeared first on Express Computer .

title: AI / Simulation Engineer
description: Responsibilities Perform EMI/EMC modeling, simulation, and analysis of electronic systems including power electronics, high‐speed digital designs, PCBs, cables, antenna, and enclosures. Use full‐wave and hybrid EM simulation tools (e.g., HFSS, CST, FEKO, SIwave, Maxwell) to evaluate conducted and radiated emissions, coupling paths, and shielding effectiveness. Provide design‐for‐EMC guidance covering PCB layout, grounding, bonding, shielding, filtering, and enclosure design. Support UL and regulatory compliance efforts by predicting emissions, identifying risk areas, and recommending mitigation strategies prior to certification testing. Assist with pre‐compliance testing, simulation correlation with lab data, and resolution of EMI/EMC test failures. Creating the V&V roadmap for different products as per UL Standard and conduct independent verification and validation and uncertainty quantification. Work closely with hardware, PCB, power electronics, product, and compliance teams to translate standards into actionable design requirements. Interface with UL labs and external test houses to support certification reviews, engineering judgments, and corrective actions. Develop technical documentation, simulation reports, and compliance evidence to support UL submissions and internal design reviews. Mentor junior engineers and contribute to internal EMI/EMC design guidelines and best practices. Qualifications BE / ME / MS in Electrical Engineering, Electronics, Electromagnetics, or a related field. 7+ years of hands‐on experience in EMI/EMC analysis, simulation, and validation for electronic systems. Strong foundation in electromagnetic theory, EMI/EMC coupling mechanisms, shielding, grounding, and signal/power integrity. Proficiency in EMI/EMC simulation tools (HFSS, CST, FEKO, SIwave, Maxwell) with demonstrated ability to correlate simulations to test data. Working knowledge of global EMI/EMC and safety standards, including CISPR, IEC 61000, ISO 11452/7637, FCC, and UL standards. Hands‐on experience providing design‐for‐EMC guidance for PCB layout, grounding, bonding, shielding, filtering, and enclosure design, with the ability to influence hardware and PCB decisions to reduce EMI/EMC risk prior to certification. Proven experience in EMI/EMC modeling, simulation, and analysis of complex electronic systems (power electronics, high‐speed digital designs, PCBs, cables, antennas, and enclosures), including identification of emission sources and coupling paths. Strong expertise in conducted and radiated emissions, including prediction, mitigation, and correlation of simulation results with laboratory test data to improve first‐pass compliance. Experience supporting products through UL certification, pre‐compliance assessment, and failure root cause analysis. Prior knowledge and experience of V&V in EMI/EMC simulation. Perform independent verification, validation, and uncertainty quantification to ensure model credibility and traceability. Establish correlation metrics between simulation and physical test data. Excellent communication skills with the ability to clearly explain complex EM concepts to cross‐functional and customer teams. Proactive mindset with interest in emerging technologies such as EVs, high‐voltage systems, and fast‐switching power electronics.
